name: Raisin Sauce Robert
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine brown sugar and water in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir until the sugar is fully dissolved, which should take about 5 minutes.
2. Add all remaining ingredients to the pan: butter, cider vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, salt, black pepper, ground cloves, mace, and currant jelly.
3. Cook the mixture on low heat, stirring occasionally, until the currant jelly has completely dissolved, which should take an additional 10 minutes.
4. The sauce can be prepared in advance and stored refrigerated until ready to use. When reheating, do so slowly over low heat until it begins to simmer.
